content.full_text:
California
Fair P01itical
Practices Cornmission
December l-l-, 199O
Jeanne C. Pavao
corporate Counsel
Diablo Construction co. , Inc.
4021 Port chicago Highway
P. O. Box 4113
Concord, CA 94524-4LL3
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-90-687
Dear !1s. Pavao:
You have requested advice on behaLf of Diablo construction
co. concerning the campaign provisions of the Political 
Reform
act.1 In our telephone conversation of November 15, 1"990, you
wj.thdrew your request for written advice for tlro of the three
questions listed in your letter.
OUESTION
Must contributions to a county supervisorial candidate made
by subcontractors of Diablo Construction co., and at the request
oi oiablo construction co., be reported on Diabto Construction
company's major donor statement?
CONCLUSION
Unless the sane person or group of persons, in fact, directs
and control,s the contributions of both DiabLo Construction Co. and
a contributi,ng subcontractor, or a subcontractor acts as an
intermediary or agent for Diablo Construction co., Diabl"o
construction company is not required to rePort the contributions
of any subcontractor.
FACTS
In a letter fron the campaign nanager of a candidate for
county supervisor, Diablo construction co., which is a general
contractor, was requested to contact its subcontractors to solicit
contributions for the candidate. Contributions from the
1 
co.r"r.,ment Code Sections 81OoO-91-015. AIl statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California code of Regulations
section 18000, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
-128 J Strcet, Suitc tloO a P.O. Rox EO7 o Srcramcn(o C.{, 95Stl.*-O8tl7 . (916) -}22-56(rL)


Jeanne C. Pavao
Page Two
subcontractors would be made directly from the subcontractor to
the candidate-
ANALYSIS
A najor donor comnittee is required to report contributions
and expenditures it makes. (section a42LL. ) contributions made
by a subcontractor of Diablo construction co. must be reported on
Diablo construction's major donor statenent if the same person or
group of persons, in fact, directs and control-s the contributions
of both the subcontractor and Diablo construction co. 
(ReguLation
!8428, copy enclosed.) Alternatively, if the subcontractor makes
the contribution on behalf of Diablo construction co. as an agent,
the contribution woul-d have to be reported by Diablo construction
co. In addition, if the subcontractor acts as an intermediary for
Diablo construction Company's contributj-ons, i.e., 
the
subcontractor makes contributions using Diablo construction Co.
funds or there is an agreement that the subcontractor's
contribution will, be reimbursed by Diablo construction Co., such
contributions must be reported by Diablo construction co.
Additionally, if Diablo construction co. incurs any costs in
soliciting its subcontractors at the behest of the candidate,
these costs must be reported by Diablo construction co. as in-kind
contributions to the candidate. (Regulation 18215.)
If you have additional questions, please contact me at (915)
322-5662.
Sincerely,
Scott HaIlabrin
Act
aI counsel
1>lz---_,
litical 
Reform consultant
Enc Iosure

QUESTION: Must contributions to a county supervisorial candidate made
by subcontractors of Diablo Construction co., and at the request
oi oiablo construction co., be reported on Diabto Construction
company's major donor statement?

CONCLUSION: Unless the sane person or group of persons, in fact, directs
and control,s the contributions of both DiabLo Construction Co. and
a contributi,ng subcontractor, or a subcontractor acts as an
intermediary or agent for Diablo Construction co., Diabl"o
construction company is not required to rePort the contributions
of any subcontractor.
